summary.summary: Replaces the previous fixed glucose fetchLimit (72 readings = 6h) with a dynamic calculation tied to preferences.maxMealAbsorptionTime. The change loads preferences earlier, then either computes a glucoseFetchLimit as Int((maxMealAbsorptionTime * 12).rounded up) (initial approach) or sets a glucoseFetchHours = maxMealAbsorptionTime + 0.5 and calls fetchAndProcessGlucose with fetchLimit: nil and fetchHours set. The fetchAndProcessGlucose signature was extended to accept a fetchHours parameter (default 24), and the glucose query now uses a time cutoff computed from fetchHours hours ago (replacing the previous one-day/fixed predicate) while still passing through a fetchLimit if provided. (See PR description and the OpenAPS.swift diffs/commits.)
summary.userFacingImpact: Glucose history supplied to the meal/COB logic is now fetched to cover the configured maxMealAbsorptionTime (plus a 0.5h buffer in the final change) instead of being limited to 6 hours. The intent is to prevent the reported COB rise and cliff-drop when users set MMAT > 6h; the change is applied upstream of determineBasal so it affects both JS oref and the native oref-swift path. The PR includes test suggestions to verify COB trajectories and glucoseFetchLimit/hours scaling.
